channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: San Diegans host rally for Ukraine following President Trump's meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: President Donald Trump met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House today to discuss ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: After Voting Rights Act case arguments, concerns over diminished minority representation rise
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the faces of American politics have grown more diverse by nearly every measure, especially as racial minority communities gained political representation after longtime legal disenfranchisement and violent discrimination.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No SNAP benefits to be issued in November as government shutdown stretches on
item.description: Local governments are warning citizens that because the federal government has been shut down for weeks with no end in sight, November SNAP benefits will not be issued.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: The test for U.S. citizenship is about to get harder
item.description: The Trump administration says more difficult questions, and other changes to the naturalization process, will ensure only immigrants who are "fully assimilated" will gain citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido, MPR News

item.title: White House joins Bluesky and immediately trolls Trump opponents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Friday joined Bluesky, the social media platform of choice of many in the left-leaning online world.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump drops the F-bomb before the cameras. It wasn't the first time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump talks plainly, bluntly, often even coarsely. But it’s rare he drops an actual F-bomb as cameras are rolling.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators see momentum on Russia sanctions even as Trump questions timing
item.description: Senate leaders push for vote on Russian sanctions as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visits Washington to press for stronger action against Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio lawmaker pushes bill to defund sanctuary cities, boost ICE cooperation
item.description: Ohio state Rep. Josh Williams is looking to use funding penalties to push sanctuary cities to cooperate with federal agencies on immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Want to vote in California's special election on redistricting? You're running out of time
item.description: With the November special election just a few weeks away, San Diego County residents are running out of time to register to vote if they haven't done so already.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to allow deployment of National Guard in Chicago area
item.description: The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area, escalating President Donald Trump’s conflict with Democratic governors over using the military on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FEMA staff sound the alarm on disaster preparedness at rally in front of agency headquarters
item.description: Current and former staff of the Federal Emergency Management Agency demonstrated against workforce and program cuts during a “FEMA Solidarity Rally” on Friday, a potentially risky act of protest because some of the same staffers were placed on leave after signing a public dissent letter in August.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP turns Schumer print quotes into AI-generated video of him saying shutdown good for Democrats
item.description: Senate Republican campaign leaders are using artificial intelligence-generated video to falsely depict Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer appearing to say on camera comments he made in a print publication interview about the political impacts of the partial government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York GOP shuts down state's Young Republicans over group chat
item.description: The New York Republican State Committee’s leaders unanimously voted to disband the Empire State’s Young Republicans chapter on Friday after screenshots from a Telegram chat exposed members using antisemitic and racist language.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: UN postpones vote on global carbon tax after Trump-led pushback
item.description: Global shipping carbon tax postponed one year following Trump administration pushback. International Maritime Organization vote delayed amid fierce U.S. resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama, aiming to boost Democrats in New Jersey and Virginia, endorses Sherrill and Spanberger
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Barack Obama has endorsed the Democratic candidates for governor in New Jersey and Virginia, seeking to boost Mikie Sherrill and Abigail Spanberger ahead of high-stakes elections next month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says 'tremendous bad blood' between Zelenskyy, Putin delaying peace deal despite Middle East momentum
item.description: President Donald Trump said "tremendous bad blood" between Zelenskyy and Putin is delaying a peace deal from happening with the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers say Trump admin is making him political pawn
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense team issued a letter to the judge arguing the Trump admin is using him as a political pawn to further its agenda.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: St. Paul mayoral candidates lay out their visions for Minnesota's capital city
item.description: Three candidates for St. Paul mayor discuss key issues in a MPR News and Pioneer Press debate. Incumbent Melvin Carter, state Rep. Kaohly Her and scientist Yan Chen address city spending, taxes, amenities and public safety.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: A war on drugs or a war on terror? Trump's military pressure on Venezuela blurs the lines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Under President Donald Trump, the drug war is looking a lot like the war on terror. To support strikes against Latin American gangs and drug cartels, the Trump administration is relying on a legal argument that gained traction after the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks, which allowed U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, ERIC TUCKER and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thune to force Democrats to vote on paying the troops during the shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) plans to force Democrats to vote next week on legislation to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown, doubling down on his strategy of highlighting the plight of military families, according to Senate GOP sources.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Bolton surrenders at Maryland courthouse
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — John Bolton, the former national security adviser-turned-foe of President Trump, surrendered himself to authorities Friday morning after he was charged with unlawfully sharing and storing classified information. Bolton arrived at the federal courthouse in Greenbelt, Md.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ties crime with immigration, blurring the lines with Guard deployment
item.description: As President Trump pushes to get National Guard troops patrolling American cities, his administration has, in effect, blurred the lines between the military, traditional law enforcement and immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Protesters clash with police outside Illinois ICE facility
item.description: Illinois State Police were seen pushing protesters back with wooden batons as a crowd gathered at the facility in Broadview, Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Senators will force a vote to prevent war on Venezuela
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia says new legislation is needed to prevent the Trump administration’s escalating actions against Venezuela from leading to war.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the New York City mayor's debate
item.description: It was the first time the candidates were back on the debate stage since Mayor Eric Adams exited the race in September.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego International Airport among airports not displaying Kristi Noem video
item.description: San Diego International Airport (SAN) has joined other airports in not displaying a video of Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Florida AG sues California, Washington over 'sanctuary' policies after deadly U-turn crash
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has filed a federal lawsuit against California and Washington over the states' "sanctuary" polices, which he claims have put others in danger.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis mayoral candidates push for limits on ICE
item.description: Three challengers for the Minneapolis mayor’s seat say they support a ban on Minneapolis police sharing information with federal immigration agencies or providing crowd control for agencies like ICE.
item.dc:creator: Jon Collins, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘We roundly condemn’ Young Republicans group chat, swastika American flag
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Thursday that Republicans “roundly condemn” the group chat among Young Republican officials that contained racist, antisemitic and homophobic comments, as well as an American flag with a swastika that was pictured behind an aide in a GOP congressman’s office.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: At least 27 states turned over sensitive data about food stamp recipients to USDA
item.description: Democratic-led states secured a legal victory to keep the personal data of food recipients out of the federal government's reach. But NPR's reporting shows that millions of records on Americans have already been shared.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Trump warns Hamas if bloodshed persists in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump is warning Hamas “we will have no choice but to go in and kill them” if internal bloodshed persists in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump to meet Putin in Hungary in effort to end 'inglorious' war
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he intends to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Hungary in the coming weeks to discuss efforts to end the war in Ukraine. Trump made the announcement following a lengthy phone call with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Furloughed air traffic controller urges lawmakers to make a deal
item.description: NewsNation Town Hall participant and FAA air traffic controller is working another job in order to make ends meet during government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Katie Porter expresses regret for tense exchange with reporter, berating staffer
item.description: Former California congresswoman Katie Porter is attempting to press "reset" on her campaign for governor following a disastrous week that started with a video of a tense exchange with a journalist and then evolved into a broader critique of her temperament when another video surfaced.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas airport won't play TSA video blaming Democrats for shutdown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The Las Vegas airport will not play a video where Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem blames Democrats for the federal government shutdown. Leaders at Harry Reid International Airport followed their counterparts at airports in Chicago and Phoenix in making their decision.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
